data.title: S&P 500 wobbles as Oracle slides, Alphabet jumps; Nasdaq hits record intraday high
data.description:
Major U.S. stock averages sought firm direction Tuesday afternoon, with Big Tech back in the spotlight as communication services stocks climbed while Oracle (ORCL) suffered following its quarterly update.

Stocks were coming off a negative session on Monday, after notching a three-week win streak. A cautious tone has been setting in ahead of consumer and wholesale inflation reports due this week.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.1% after scaling a fresh intraday record high of 19,887.08 points. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) -0.1% after notching small gains. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was flat in afternoon trade.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.23%, and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was also up 3 basis points to 4.16%.

The S&P 500's (SP500) Communication Services sector +2.4%, pacing gains in five other sectors, bolstered by Alphabet. Class A (GOOGL) and class C (GOOG) shares of the tech titan rose +5%, moving up a day after the company revealed a powerful quantum computing chip.

But Oracle (ORCL) on the index sank -7.7% Tuesday after the cloud software company disappointed investors with fiscal second-quarter results that slightly missed expectations.

Traders will receive the consumer price inflation for November on Wednesday, ahead of next week's Federal Reserve monetary policy committee meeting, the last of the year. The core consumer price index is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M, and 3.3% Y/Y.

The headline number is forecast to come in at 0.2% on a monthly basis, and 2.7% annually.

"That release (CPI) is going to be a crucial one, as it’s probably the last big piece of the jigsaw ahead of the Fed’s policy decision next week," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In other stock movers, developer data platform MongoDB (MDB) -15% despite delivering a top- and bottom-line beat and lifting its annual revenue and adjusted profit guidance. Michael Gordon, MongoDB's CFO and COO for nearly a decade, will retire in January.

In Tuesday's economic calendar, Q3 labor costs were revised down, while the productivity number stayed at 2.2%.

data.title: Apple to bring satellite connectivity, other changes, to Apple Watch next year: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is planning to make several changes to several models of the Apple Watch next year, including satellite connectivity, Bloomberg reported.

Shares of the tech giant rose fractionally in midday trading, while Globalstar (NYSE:GSAT), its satellite connectivity partner, rose 8.8%.

The new connectivity feature is set to come to the high-end version of the watch, the Apple Watch Ultra, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The feature, first introduced as part of the iPhone 14 lineup in 2022, allows people to send messages and calls when they do not have Wi-Fi or cellular connectivity. The emergency service was included free for two years, but so far, Apple has not charged for the service.

Last month, Globalstar shares jumped after it announced an expanded deal with Apple (AAPL) that saw Apple make an infrastructure prepayment of up to $1.1B and take a 20% stake in the company.

In addition, Apple is increasing its focus on a blood-pressure feature for the Watch and could arrive as soon as next year, Bloomberg added. Apple has worked on the feature for some time now, though it has been marred by delays.

The Apple Watch may also get an internal change, with some models having modems built by MediaTek, replacing those made by Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), Bloomberg added. Apple is working on in-house modems for the iPhone in an effort to rely less on external partners and control more of its devices.

Intel shares fell 3% in late afternoon trading.

Apple and Intel did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Phillips 66 partners with NextEra to power Rodeo refinery with solar energy
data.description:
Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) said Tuesday it will power its Rodeo refinery in California with a new 30.2 MW solar facility owned and operated by NextEra Energy (NEE) (NEP), making it one of the largest on-site dedicated solar facilities in the U.S.; financial details were not disclosed.

The solar plant will be built on 88 acres of land owned by Phillips 66 (PSX), adjacent to its renewable fuels facility in the San Francisco Bay Area, and will consist of more than 70K solar modules that will support the renewable fuels complex.

The facility, which is designed to generate ~60K MWh/year of electricity, is expected to reduce the refinery's grid power demand by 50% and lower its carbon dioxide emissions by 33K metric tons/year starting in Q1 2025, Phillips 66 (PSX) said.

The company converted the refinery to producing renewable diesel from fats, vegetable oils and greases, after previously making gasoline and diesel from crude oil; the complex began processing only renewable feedstocks in March, achieving full processing capacity of 50K bbl/day in June.

data.title: The public companies with the 'most bored' workforces
data.description:
Telling your boss that your job is boring is not a great idea, but an online casino games site has gauged which Fortune 500 companies could perhaps use an injection of fun and entertainment.

PlayUSA cheekily set its sights on Fortune 500 companies in sifting through +2.4M Glassdoor reviews to analyze where are the “most bored employees” in America. “[We] know how beneficial online casino games can be to offer a quick brain break anytime throughout the workday,” was the angle from PlayUSA.

The list of the top 100 major companies with the “most bored workforce” was compiled with PlayUSA specifically searching for reviews that mentioned keywords about experiencing boredom. RTX (RTX), which is formerly Raytheon, grocer Albertsons (ACI), and insurer Progressive (PGR) landed atop the list.

“With a cool name like ‘aerospace and defense,’ you probably wouldn’t have guessed that the industry ranked as the most boring to work in, with an average bored score of 92.42,” PlayUSA said. “Although this industry focuses on the design, development, and production of all different types of air and spacecraft, it’s apparently not always as fun as it would seem.”

Ouch.

Here’s PlayUSA’s full list – feel free to take with several tablespoons of salt, read it with amusement or disregard altogether. Below are the top 10 publicly traded companies on the list with their industries, PlayUSA’s “Bored Score” from 0-100, and SA’s Quant Ratings:

  1. RTX (RTX) - Aerospace & Defense - Bored Score: 98.26. SA Quant Rating: U4.83
  2. Albertsons (ACI) - Retail - Bored Score: 97.64. SA Quant Rating: 2.90
  3. Progressive (PGR) - Insurance - Bored Score: 97.10. SA Quant Rating: 4.87
  4. U.S. Bancorp (USB) - Banking - Bored Score: 94.06. SA Quant Rating: 3.45
  5. Paccar (PCAR) - Automotive - Bored Score: 93.80. SA Quant Rating: 3.04
  6. TJX (TJX) - Retail - Bored Score: 93.45. SA Quant Rating: 4.83
  7. Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) - Finance. Bored Score: 93.45. SA Quant Rating: 4.97
  8. Travelers (TRV) - Insurance. Bored Score: 92.95. SA Quant Rating: 4.77
  9. Elevance Health (ELV) - Insurance. Bored Score: 92.76. SA Quant Rating: 2.75
  10. Spectrum - part of Charter (CHTR) - Telecommunications - Bored Score: 92.42. SA Quant Rating: 3.34

data.title: Jefferies says the Russell 2000 will reach 2,715 by 2025 year-end
data.description:
Jefferies analysts see the Russell 2000 (NYSEARCA:IWM) gaining 13.5% to 2,715 by the end of 2025, “finally beating the large caps after 8 long years of underperformance.”

In an equity strategy note, Steven DeSanctis, Jefferies U.S. small-mid cap strategist, said that analysts also see the 2025 earnings growing at 13% “slightly lower than current forecast (16%) but ahead of large caps.”

Small caps (IWM) continue to be less expensive than large caps (SP500). “Since June 30, valuations have risen with the forward P/E at 23.5x and in the 95th percentile,” he said. “Our absolute valuation model is in the 88th percentile vs. 63rd at the start of the year. This tends to mean weak absolute performance going forward.”

“Given the fact that small caps are cheap, and balance sheets are clean, along with less headwinds from D.C. under the new administration, M&A activity should remain strong, continue to broaden out, and accelerate in ’25,” he continued.

DeSanctis also explained that three stocks alone in the S&P 500 (SP500) are above $3T – Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and Microsoft (MSFT) – and two stocks are bigger than the entire Russell 2000 (IWM).

“This puts the top five names at 4.4x the entire Russell 2000 (IWM), very close to a record,” he said. “The average price-to-sales ratio of the ‘Top 5’ is 12.7x, median is at 9.4x, compared to 2x for the Russell 2000 (IWM). The gap in relative price-to-sales is now at 6.4x.”

And despite the broadening of the market, the top 10 Russell 2000 stocks weigh in at 33.3% of the index, which is close to an all-time high.

data.title: Matthews International edges higher as activist pushes for CEO exit, board seats (update)
data.description:
Update 2:20pm: Adds Matthews response.

Matthews International (MATW) rose 3.7% as activist holder Barington Capital pushed for the casket-maker to replace its CEO, sell off underperforming business and nominate three board directors.

Barington Capital, which owns a ~2% stake in Matthews (NASDAQ:MATW), wants the company to start a search for a new CEO to replace Joe Bartolacci, according to a letter the company sent to Matthews board on Tuesday.

"We are patient, long-term investors, but we can no longer accept the status quo," Barington's James Mitarotonda wrote in the letter. "We strongly believe that Matthews needs to take decisive steps to address its prolonged period of share price underperformance and unlock its value potential."

Matthews said in October it attempted to continue consulting with Mitarotonda about the company’s evaluation of its portfolio and Mitarotonda refused, the company said in a statement on Tuesday. Matthews also highlighted that it announced in its most recent results that it hired JPMorgan to support the evaluation of potential strategic alternatives for its portfolio of businesses.

"Now that the agreement with Barington has expired, rather than engage in a private dialogue with our Board and potentially exploring a constructive path forward, Barington has decided to wage a costly and disruptive proxy contest," Matthew said in the statement.

Barington has been an investor in Matthews since 2022 and the hedge fund pushed for a refreshed board in December of that year.

"Based on the Company’s performance during Mr. Bartolacci’s extended 18-year tenure, we do not believe that he is the right leader to unlock Matthews’ significant value potential," Mitarotonda added.

Barington is calling for Matthew's to explore the sale of its "poorly performing" packaging business, SGK Brand Solutions.

data.title: MasterCraft and Chevy Silverado announce marketing partnership
data.description:
• MasterCraft Boat Company (NASDAQ:MCFT) is leveraging the Chevrolet (GM) brand to raise awareness of the boat manufacturer at MasterCraft’s (MCFT) premier events through an exclusive partnership, naming the Chevy Silverado as its “Official Truck & Tow Vehicle” for 2025 and beyond.
  • As part of its collaboration with Chevy (GM), MasterCraft (MCFT) will receive eight state-of-the-art Silverado 2500HD pick-up trucks to be prominently displayed at 32 boat shows, auctions, golf tournaments, and various dealer-facing programs.
  • The choice of the Chevy Silverado as its marketing partner was based on the connection between the 5.3L GDI HO engine in MasterCraft (MCFT) boats and the Silverado.
  • Nearly identical to the available 5.3L engine of the LD Silverado, the marine version for MasterCraft (MCFT) is adapted by Ilmor, MasterCraft’s (MCFT) exclusive engine partner.
  • MasterCraft (MCFT) shares perked up on the announcement, erasing an earlier loss to trade with a modest gain. General Motors (GM) shares are unchanged.
